competency_weightage.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding by organizing, comparing, translating.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts. Make inferences. Present and defend opinions. Compile information together.

Maintenance of practical file and oral examination.

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Quantitative Chemical Analysis and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Ability to measure physical quantities with uncertainty, apply laws of chemical combination, perform stoichiometric calculations using the mole concept, and analyze percentage composition.
competencies: Measure properties of matter with appropriate units and quantify uncertainty in measurements
Apply Laws of Chemical Combination (Conservation of Mass, Definite/Multiple Proportions)
Utilize Dalton's Atomic Theory to explain chemical phenomena
Calculate atomic/molecular masses and apply the Mole Concept for quantitative analysis
Perform stoichiometric calculations based on balanced chemical equations

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Understanding the evolution from classical models to quantum mechanical descriptions, including sub-atomic particles, electronic configurations, and spectral properties.
competencies: Identify sub-atomic particles (electron, proton, neutron) and their discovery history
Evaluate historical atomic models (Thomson, Rutherford, Bohr) and their limitations
Apply the Quantum Mechanical Model to describe electron behavior in atoms
Interpret hydrogen atom spectra using quantum numbers

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify elements based on electronic configuration, understand the Modern Periodic Law, and predict properties using periodic trends.
competencies: Justify the need for classification of elements and trace its historical development
Apply the Modern Periodic Law to organize elements by atomic number
Determine block classifications (s, p, d, f) based on electronic configuration
Predict periodic trends in physical and chemical properties across periods and groups
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Geometry
competency_groups.description: Understanding the formation, nature, and geometry of chemical bonds using various theoretical approaches.
competencies: Explain bond formation through Kossel-Lewis approach (ionic/covalent)
Analyze bond parameters (length, energy, angle) to determine molecular stability
Predict molecular shapes using VSEPR theory and hybridization concepts
Differentiate between Valence Bond Theory and Molecular Orbital Theory

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamic Principles and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Application of thermodynamic laws to analyze energy changes, enthalpy variations, and criteria for spontaneity in chemical processes.
competencies: Define system types (open, closed, isolated) and state functions
Calculate internal energy change ($\Delta U$) and enthalpy change ($\Delta H$) via calorimetry
Determine the spontaneity of reactions using Gibbs Energy Change ($\Delta G$)
Relate equilibrium conditions to thermodynamic parameters

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium Dynamics and Ionic Systems
competency_groups.description: Understanding dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, including factors affecting shift (Le Chatelier's principle) and ionic equilibria.
competencies: Distinguish between homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria
Calculate equilibrium constants ($K$) and relate them to reaction quotient ($Q$)
Predict shifts in equilibrium position due to changes in concentration, pressure, or temperature (Le Chatelier's Principle)
Analyze ionic equilibria involving acids, bases, buffers, and solubility products

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Ability to identify oxidation-reduction processes, balance redox equations using oxidation numbers, and understand electrode reactions.
competencies: Define oxidation and reduction in terms of electron transfer and oxygen/hydrogen gain-loss
Assign oxidation numbers to elements in compounds and ions
Balance complex redox equations using the ion-electron method or oxidation number method
Relate redox reactions to electrode processes

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Understanding the structure, nomenclature, isomerism, and reaction mechanisms of organic compounds.
competencies: Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules to name complex organic structures
Identify different types of isomers (structural, stereoisomers)
Analyze fundamental concepts in organic reaction mechanisms (substitution, addition, elimination)
Perform qualitative and quantitative analysis for functional groups
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify hydrocarbons, understand their preparation methods, physical properties, and characteristic chemical reactions.
competencies: Differentiate between alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic hydrocarbons
Predict products of addition reactions for unsaturated hydrocarbons
Explain the mechanism of electrophilic substitution in benzene derivatives
Assess health hazards (carcinogenicity) associated with specific hydrocarbon classes

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Understanding the expression of concentration, solubility principles, and calculation of molar masses using colligative properties.
competencies: Express solution concentrations in various units (molarity, molality, mole fraction)
Apply Raoult's Law to ideal and non-ideal solutions
Calculate molecular mass from depression in freezing point or elevation in boiling point
Explain the phenomenon of abnormal molecular masses

competency_groups.title: Electrochemical Cells and Applications
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze galvanic and electrolytic cells, apply the Nernst equation, and understand industrial applications like batteries and corrosion.
competencies: Differentiate between Galvanic (voltaic) and Electrolytic cells
Calculate cell potential using the Nernst Equation under non-standard conditions
Explain principles of conductance in electrolytic solutions
Analyze mechanisms of corrosion, batteries, and fuel cells

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates
competency_groups.description: Understanding factors affecting reaction rates, deriving integrated rate laws, and applying collision theory.
competencies: Define rate of reaction and determine it experimentally
Analyze the effect of concentration, temperature, and catalysts on reaction rate
Derive and apply zero-order and first-order integrated rate equations
Explain the Temperature Dependence of reaction rates using Arrhenius equation concepts

competency_groups.title: d- and f-Block Element Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Understanding the electronic configuration, general properties, and applications of transition elements and inner transition metals.
competencies: Explain the position of d-block and f-block elements in the periodic table
Correlate electronic configurations with magnetic and catalytic properties
Describe important compounds and oxidation states of Transition Elements (d-Block)
Differentiate between Lanthanoids and Actinoids

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry and Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Ability to name coordination compounds, understand isomerism, and apply bonding theories (Valence Bond, Crystal Field).
competencies: Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules for complex ions and neutral complexes
Identify different types of isomers in coordination compounds (geometric, optical)
Explain Werner's Theory of Coordination Compounds
Analyze bonding nature using Valence Bond Theory and Crystal Field Theory

competency_groups.title: Organic Halogen Derivatives
competency_groups.description: Understanding the nomenclature, preparation methods, and chemical reactivity of haloalkanes and haloarenes.
competencies: Distinguish between alkyl halides (haloalkanes) and aryl halides (haloarenes)
Predict products of nucleophilic substitution reactions
Analyze the nature of C-X bond polarity and its effect on reactivity
Identify polyhalogen compounds and their uses

competency_groups.title: Oxygen-Containing Organic Compounds (Alcohols, Phenols, Ethers)
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify oxygen-containing compounds, understand their preparation methods, and distinguish chemical properties.
competencies: Differentiate between alcohols, phenols, and ethers based on structure
Apply IUPAC nomenclature for substituted derivatives
Predict reactions involving dehydration of alcohols and oxidation processes
Explain the acidity differences between phenols and aliphatic alcohols

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Compounds: Aldehydes and Ketones
competency_groups.description: Understanding the structure of carbonyl group, preparation methods, nucleophilic addition reactions, and uses.
competencies: Distinguish between aldehydes and ketones based on functional groups
Predict products of nucleophilic addition reactions (e.g., with HCN, Grignard reagents)
Identify oxidation methods for converting primary/secondary alcohols to carbonyls
Recognize uses of common aldehydes and ketones in industry

competency_groups.title: Carboxylic Acids: Structure and Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Ability to name carboxylic acids, understand their acidic nature, preparation methods, and derivatives.
competencies: Apply IUPAC nomenclature for monocarboxylic and dicarboxylic acids
Explain the acidity of carboxylic acids compared to alcohols and phenols
Predict reactions involving decarboxylation and esterification
Identify methods for preparing carboxylic acids from various precursors

competency_groups.title: Nitrogen-Containing Organic Compounds (Amines)
competency_groups.description: Understanding the classification, nomenclature, basicity of amines, and synthesis via diazonium salts.
competencies: Classify primary, secondary, and tertiary amines
Explain the basic nature of amines relative to ammonia
Synthesize aromatic compounds using Diazonium salt chemistry (Sandmeyer reaction)
Identify physical properties distinguishing different classes of amines

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules and Biochemical Processes
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify biomolecules, understand their structures (monomers/polymers), and biological functions.
competencies: Classify carbohydrates as monosaccharides, disaccharides, or polysaccharides
Describe the structure of proteins (amino acids) and enzymes

question_paper_design: No chapter-wise weightage provided; care taken to cover all chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
No overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices given in all sections.

competency_weightage.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding by organizing, comparing, translating.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts. Make inferences. Present and defend opinions. Compile information together.

internal_assessment.policy: 33% internal choices provided in all sections of the question paper.
internal_assessment.variations: Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.

practical_assessment.description: Includes preparation of standard solutions and titrations (e.g., KMnO4 vs Oxalic acid).
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt.
Specific experiments based on syllabus content (e.g., pH studies, equilibrium shifts).
Maintenance of practical file and oral examination.

project_assessment.requirements: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources. Approval by teacher required for non-prescribed projects.
